revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsukayama Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[kofun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[Spindle (textiles)|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Banetsu West Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Aizu-otsukayama-kofun}} *[https://www.aizukanko.com/spot/41 Aizuwakamatsu home page] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[Category:History of Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Aizuwakamatsu]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]

The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[Spindle (textiles)|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. {{Short description|Historic site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsukayama Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[Zenpokoenfun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[Spindle (textiles)|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Banetsu West Line]].

{{Short description|Historic site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsukayama Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[Zenpokoenfun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[Spindle (textiles)|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Banetsu West Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Historic site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsukayama Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[Zenpokoenfun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[Spindle (textiles)|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Banetsu West Line]].

{{Short description|Historic site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsukayama Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[Zenpokoenfun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[Spindle (textiles)|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Banetsu West Line]].

The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Banetsu West Line]].

{{Short description|Historic site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōtsukayama Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[Zenpokoenfun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[Spindle (textiles)|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.

==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[Spindle (textiles)|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Banetsu West Line]].

{{Short description|Historic site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōtsukayama Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[Zenpokoenfun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[Spindle (textiles)|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Banetsu West Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Historic site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōtsukayama Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[Zenpokoenfun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[Spindle (textiles)|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Banetsu West Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Historic site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōtsukayama Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[Zenpokoenfun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[Spindle (textiles)|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Banetsu West Line]].

==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[:en:Spindle (textiles)#Whorl|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[:en:Fukushima Museum|Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Banetsu West Line|Banetsu West Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Aizu-otsukayama-kofun]][[en:Ōtsukayama Kofun]][[ja:会津大塚山古墳]][[zh:會津大塚山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11381233]] Wikidata name: Ōtsukayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Historic site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōtsukayama Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima|Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima|Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property of Japan|Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[:en:Spindle (textiles)#Whorl|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[:en:Fukushima Museum|Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Banetsu West Line|Banetsu West Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Aizu-otsukayama-kofun]][[en:Ōtsukayama Kofun]][[ja:会津大塚山古墳]][[zh:會津大塚山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11381233]] Wikidata name: Ōtsukayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Historic site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōtsukayama Kofun | native_name = 大塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima|Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima|Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property of Japan|Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[:en:Spindle (textiles)#Whorl|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[:en:Fukushima Museum|Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Banetsu West Line|Banetsu West Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

part of the city of [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima|Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property of Japan|Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[:en:Spindle (textiles)#Whorl|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[:en:Fukushima Museum|Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Banetsu West Line|Banetsu West Line]].

of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|key-hole shaped tumulus]] located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima|Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property of Japan|Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included [[:en:Spindle (textiles)#Whorl|spindles]], cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the [[:en:Fukushima Museum|Fukushima Museum]] in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Banetsu West Line|Banetsu West Line]].

period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=key-hole shaped tumulus}} located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima|Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property of Japan|Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

native_name_lang = | image = 会津大塚山古墳.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|30|46|N|139|56|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima|Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōtsukayama Kofun'''|大塚山古墳|Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun}} is an early [[Kofun period]] ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=key-hole shaped tumulus}} located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima|Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima]] in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property of Japan|Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included {{ill|Spindle (textiles)#Whorl|en|Spindle (textiles)#Whorl|WD=Q213761|lt=spindles}}, cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the {{ill|Fukushima Museum|en|Fukushima Museum|WD=Q4352239|lt=Fukushima Museum}} in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|en|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|WD=Q4699462|lt=Aizu-Wakamatsu Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Banetsu West Line|Banetsu West Line]].
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209660 |title=大塚山古墳|trans-title= Ōtsukayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the second largest kofun in Fukushima Prefecture after the [[Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori kofun]] and the fourth largest in the Tōhoku region. The {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property of Japan|Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== [[File:会津大塚山古墳南棺出土 三角縁唐草文帯三神二獣鏡.JPG|right|thumb|Bronze Mirror recovered from the Ōtsukayama ''kofun'', a National ICP]] The Ōtsukayama ''kofun'' is located in the southeastern corner of the Aizu Basin, at a height of 30 meters on the summit of Mount Otsukayama. It has a total length of 114 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 54 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 70 meters with a height of 10 meters. The domed portion was built in three tiers and the rectangular portion in two tiers, and it dates from the early Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century).
The tomb was first excavated in 1964, during which time only the domed portion was opened. A [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with two wooden bamboo-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} was found. A large number of artifacts were recovered from the southern burial vicinity, including a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, fragments of swords, armor and whetstones, for a total of 279 items. The northern sarcophagus vicinity included {{ill|Spindle (textiles)#Whorl|en|Spindle (textiles)#Whorl|WD=Q213761|lt=spindles|1=Spindle (textiles)}}, cylindrical beads and knives, for a total of 95 items. The artifacts date the ''kofun'' to the 6th century AD indicate a strong connection to the Yamato Court. Many of these items are on display at the {{ill|Fukushima Museum|en|Fukushima Museum|WD=Q4352239|lt=Fukushima Museum|1=Fukushima Museum}} in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|en|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|WD=Q4699462|lt=Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|1=Aizu-Wakamatsu Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Banetsu West Line|Banetsu West Line]].
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}

Many of these items are on display at the {{ill|Fukushima Museum|en|Fukushima Museum|WD=Q4352239|lt=Fukushima Museum|1=Fukushima Museum}} in Aizuwakamatsu.
The site is located about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|en|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|WD=Q4699462|lt=Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|1=Aizu-Wakamatsu Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Banetsu West Line|Banetsu West Line]].

The {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property of Japan|Important Cultural Property of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187522 |title=会津大塚山古墳出土品|trans-title= Aizu-Ōtsukayama Kofun Artifacts|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
